title: Charles Mouton House, First and Second Floor Plans (HABS LA-210, sheet 3)
era_or_year: house 19th century; HABS drawings documented after 1933 (2016 Peterson Prize entry)
creator: Historic American Buildings Survey (Charles E. Peterson Prize competition entry)
research_notes: Plan is sheet 3 (00003v.jpg) of HABS LA-210 'Drawings'. Sheet 2 (00002v.jpg) of the same item is a SITE PLAN with a plant-species legend table showing the Main House plus a Garage House (multi-building) if a site/legend plan is desired.
research_features: Combined sheet with BOTH 1st-floor and 2nd-floor plans side by side; richly labeled rooms (Kitchen, Sunroom, Dining, Living, Music, Office, Atchafalaya Room, Napoleon Suite, Voorhies Suite), deep galleries/porches, full dimensions.
feature_highlights: combined sheet with first-floor and second-floor plans side by side
deep Creole galleries (colonnaded porches) ringing both long sides
richly named rooms (Living, Music, Dining, Office, Sunroom, Kitchen; Napoleon and Voorhies Suites, Atchafalaya Room, Sitting Room)
regular rows of square porch columns with per-bay dimensions
central stair and hallway linking the two stacked floors
stacked washer/dryer service zone and HVAC closet
north arrow and graphic scale annotations
supported_examples: rectangular and straight-edged rooms derived from walls
linear point-to-point dimensions in imperial units
roadmap_examples: multi-floor (two stacked levels on one sheet)
furniture and fixture placement (washer/dryer, plumbing fixtures)

gap_features.why: the deep Creole galleries are roofed but largely open colonnaded porches that need to be drawn as covered outdoor space rather than fully wall-enclosed rooms
